title: Scotland: Crail (Conta's Wedding)

boundary_flags: mixed_end: true — diary page B_0361 may contain content from a subsequent trip
summary: A trip to Crail in Fife, Scotland for the wedding of Conta (a family friend), who had rented a house for guests to stay in. The Saturday wedding was held at The Cow Shed at Crail, followed by a post-wedding brunch on Sunday before the return home to Birmingham.
extraction_notes: Only one diary page (B_0361_L) covers this trip. The boundary_mixed flag on this page indicates the page may run into the next trip. Dates are month-precision only (2022-03 based on date_guess; Welsh daffodils brought as a gift suggest late winter/early spring). The transit breakfast stop '[Chorby?] Woods' on the outward journey has an uncertain transcription and cannot be geocoded — logged as unresolved rather than a main_route stop. 'The Crushed' (Sunday brunch venue) is possibly a mis-transcription of 'The Cow Shed'; treated as a Crail-area excursion with low confidence. No family member names are explicitly listed for this trip in the diary text; 'we' implies Simon and Eve at minimum. No ferry crossings — trip is entirely within Great Britain.
